subject: Geography
title: The Andes: World's Longest Range
description: A fast breakdown of The Andes: World's Longest Range for builders.

hook.text: The Andes stretch 7,000 km along South America's west coast—the longest range on Earth.

buildup.text: They formed as the Pacific plate dives under South America, pushing rock skyward.

discovery.text: The Inca Empire built Machu Picchu at 2,430 meters, hidden above the cloud line.

twist.text: Lake Titicaca sits at 3,812 meters in the Andes—the highest navigable lake on Earth.

climax.text: Andean peoples adapted to thin air over thousands of years with larger lungs and hearts.

punchline.text: The longest mountain chain made the toughest people.

quiz.question: How long are the Andes Mountains?
